methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Quilter is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Sergiy Nesterenko that has raised $15M+ to date. The valuation analysis incorporates a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public company (277810.KQ), including recent-stage peers such as Foundation Robotics ($3.0B, Series A), 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), and Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis uses the $15M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $30M. Peer-median analysis across seven same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.6B, with observed range of $300M to $10.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics subsector focus, funding velocity, and commercialization progress across the peer set.

Triangulating these two methods yields a low bound of $26M, median of $840M, and high bound of $1.9B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty in how Quilter's current product maturity and addressable market compare to the peer cohort, which spans early Series A through Series B stages. Stage-progression implies near-term valuation floors; peer multiples anchor longer-term potential. Key sensitivities include technology differentiation relative to Foundation Robotics and 1X, path to Series B fundraising, and near-term revenue or deployment milestones.
